input_messages.content: {"suggestions": ["Implement ICMP rate-limiting on cisco-router-R2", "Implement ICMP rate-limiting on UbuntuComputer using iptables", "Re-configure ICMP rate-limiting on the FortiGate firewall with a lower bandwidth threshold"], "chosen_mitigation": "Implement ICMP rate-limiting on cisco-router-R2", "no_mitigation_left": false, "current_mitigations_counter": 8, "reached_mitigation_limit": false}

```json
{
  "mitigation_currently_implemented": "Implement ICMP rate-limiting on cisco-router-R2",
  "comments": "I have successfully implemented ICMP rate-limiting on the 'cisco-router-R2' device. This was achieved by configuring a rate-limit on the FastEthernet0/0 interface, which is the interface connected to the internal network. The rate-limit is set to 8000 bits per second, which should be sufficient to allow legitimate ICMP traffic while dropping the excessive traffic from the ICMP flood. This will effectively mitigate the attack at the network edge.",
  "device_identifier": "cisco-router-R2",
  "commands": [
    "configure terminal",
    "rate-limit input icmp 8000 1500 1500 conform-action transmit exceed-action drop",
    "interface fastethernet0/0",
    "rate-limit input 8000 1500 1500 conform-action transmit exceed-action drop",
    "end"
  ]
}
```

response.content: {"comment": "The implementation is incorrect. The `rate-limit` command on Cisco IOS requires an access-list to specify the traffic to be limited. The first command, `rate-limit input icmp ...`, is syntactically invalid in the global configuration context where it was used. The second command, `rate-limit input 8000 ...`, applied to the interface, will indiscriminately rate-limit *all* incoming traffic to a very low 8kbps, not just the intended ICMP traffic. This approach will cause significant collateral damage by dropping legitimate non-ICMP traffic and is therefore not an acceptable solution. A proper implementation would define an access-list to match ICMP traffic and then apply the rate-limit to that specific access group on the interface. The mitigation is not approved.", "approved": false, "mitigation_currently_implemented": "Implement ICMP rate-limiting on cisco-router-R2", "mitigations_counter": 8, "mitigation_iteration": 1, "reached_mitigation_iteration_limit": false}
